title: Plagiospermum uniflorum
intro: Plagiospermum uniflorum is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Plagiospermum uniflorum include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in the production of herbal extracts and capsules, making it a popular ingredient in herbal supplements.
